summary.pending: 0.10 — STATUS.md commit (blocked on Guide approval of proposed content below)

item.notes: Initial run blocked by S4U LogonType on 'Agents Cadence Monitor' in non-elevated shell. Lead Architect manually disabled in elevated PowerShell. Re-verify confirmed 13/13 Disabled.

item.reason: Awaiting Guide approval of proposed STATUS.md content

d0dc1c7 (no commits added on chore/platform-extraction yet — branch points at the pause commit, the desired Stage 0 anchor)

current_state_update.head: d0dc1c7 (pause-fleet commit; matches main)
current_state_update.fleet_status: Paused (fleet_paused=true, reason "platform separation v2 in progress"); 13/13 scheduled tasks Disabled

anomaly.title: mcpServers key path
anomaly.what_was_different: XML command reads `$cfg.servers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS`. Actual key in `.mcp.json` is `mcpServers` (capitalized, plural).
anomaly.what_was_done: Used `$cfg.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS`. Literal was read and persisted to User env (length 23).
anomaly.why_safe: Read-only access to the JSON config; no mutation of `.mcp.json`. Functional outcome identical to XML intent.
anomaly.follow_up: Post-procedure review of stage XML 0.0.5 command block. Stage XMLs are locked during execution.

anomaly.title: Vikunja CLI env-injection deviation
anomaly.actual_command: $env:VIKUNJA_PASS = (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\.mcp.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json).mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS; python tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py list-projects | Select-String "Fleet Reports"
anomaly.reason_for_adjustment: `tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py` reads `VIKUNJA_PASS` from the process environment at startup. In the active shell (which had not been relaunched since 0.0.5 persisted the variable to User scope) the variable was not present in `$env:`, so the CLI would have failed authentication. A one-shot session-scoped injection from the same `.mcp.json` literal made the CLI invocation succeed without modifying any persisted state.
anomaly.semantic_equivalence: The injected value is byte-identical to the literal that the MCP runtime would have supplied; the CLI behavior, output, and the captured evidence (`Fleet Reports` project_id=8) are identical to running it after a shell relaunch.
anomaly.follow_up: None — the User-scoped env var will be picked up by future shells naturally; no XML change recommended.

anomaly.title: Agents Cadence Monitor S4U elevation requirement
anomaly.what_was_different: 12 of 13 fleet tasks disabled cleanly in non-elevated PowerShell. `Agents Cadence Monitor` failed with "Access is denied" because its LogonType is **S4U** (Service for User), while the other 12 are **Interactive**. S4U tasks require an elevated shell to modify.
anomaly.what_was_done: Lead Architect opened elevated PowerShell and ran `Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' -TaskName 'Agents Cadence Monitor'`. Verified `State=Disabled`. All 13 tasks now Disabled.
anomaly.why_safe: LogonType was not changed — preserves S4U property for post-cutover behavior. No semantic change to the task.
anomaly.implication_for_stage_4: `Unregister-ScheduledTask` + `Register-ScheduledTask -Xml` for `Agents Cadence Monitor` at Stage 4.7 will also require elevation. The Stage 4 Executor MUST open an elevated shell for that task's re-registration, OR pre-disable in non-elevated shell and elevate only for Unregister+Register. Do NOT enter Stage 4's destructive block without anticipating this.
anomaly.follow_up: Post-soak investigation of why `Agents Cadence Monitor` is S4U while every other fleet task is Interactive. May be intentional (cadence flipper needs to fire regardless of login state). Capture rationale in a DevPlatform-Meta Vikunja ticket per Stage 6.7.5 pattern.

halt_directive.action_pending: Commit proposed content above to docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md.
halt_directive.blocked_on: Guide approval of proposed content (relayed via Lead Architect).
halt_directive.do_not: Do NOT modify STATUS.md until approval is relayed.
